name: Pasta Primavera
recipeIngredient: 6 quarts water for cooking the pasta
1/2 pound fresh asparagus, pencil-shaped, cut into 3-inch pieces
2 1/2 pounds fresh pasta, 3 cups dried pasta
1 cup fresh or frozen tiny green peas, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring the pasta water to a boil in a large, covered saucepan and turn the heat down to warm.
2. Steam vegetables except mushrooms, tomatoes, and peas with lemon zest until they are easily pierced with a sharp knife. Rinse with cold water, drain, and set aside.
3. Reheat the pasta water to a hard boil, add a pinch of salt, and cook the pasta until al dente (just soft enough so it still needs chewing). Fresh pasta takes 3-5 minutes; dry pasta up to 15 minutes. Drain in a colander.
4. In a large skillet over medium-low heat, cook garlic and red pepper flakes for 1-2 minutes in half the oil.
5. Turn the heat up to medium-high, add mushrooms, tomatoes, and peas, and sauté for 3 more minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Add blanched vegetables and chopped herb, tossing to mix until heated through. If cooked before pasta, remove from heat until pasta is drained and ready.
7. Transfer pasta to a large heated serving bowl or platter and toss with remaining oil.
8. Add vegetables and 1/2 cup grated Parmesan, tossing to mix.
